patch.value:
## Agent need
Multi-agent operation is pm's primary mode: many agents mutate the same git-native tracker at once and must never clobber each other or hand two agents the same work.

## Delivered surface (pm-dj98)
core/lock ownership + lock-gc; pm claim/release. In flight: atomic claim (pm-8t5x), lock-contention auto-retry (pm-2muu). Tracked edges: singleton-file races (pm-bgcu), id TOCTOU (pm-khdq), stale-lock takeover (pm-zwib).

## Verified by
Concurrency stress specs. Enduring agent-value statement for the concurrency capability; hardening lands in pm-dj98.
As one of many parallel agents, I want atomic claim and lock semantics with bounded auto-retry, so that we each get distinct work and never corrupt shared tracker state

pm claim is an atomic test-and-set (no silent steal); lock contention auto-retries with bounded jitter before lock_conflict; singleton-file writes are lock-safe; id allocation is collision-free under parallel creates.
Baseline delivered under pm-dj98 (lock ownership/gc + claim/release); atomic claim (pm-8t5x) and lock retry (pm-2muu) in flight; race hardening tracked by open children.
Parallel agents each get distinct work and never corrupt shared state.
Lock + claim/release shipped; atomicity and retry hardening tracked by open children.
Baseline delivered under pm-dj98 (lock ownership/gc + claim/release); atomic claim (pm-8t5x) and lock retry (pm-2muu) in flight; race hardening tracked by open children.
